other_observers: Peter Gent, Bob Righter, Walter Szeliga, Andrew Spencer, Christian Nunes; Andrew Spencer found this bird for us today, but see under "Other Dates."
description: Bird was swimming low in the water among Western Grebes and diving frequently. It was about the same length in the water as the Western Grebes but had a very different head and neck shape: a much thicker, shorter neck and a larger head, with a longish, thin, slightly upturned bill frequently held above the horizontal. The bird.s back was plain dusky grayish, at least as far as I could tell from the distance I was viewing from; its head and neck were slightly paler, smudgy all over, palest on the throat area; there was no clear demarcation at all between the darker crown and hindneck and the paler foreneck, and this is why I think it was a juvenile. Once while the bird was facing me in the water it reared back and flapped its wings a few times; I could see that the wings were mostly pale underneath with an ill-defined dusky border all around the pale area; the bird definitely did not have the white secondaries of a grebe.Behaviors: Bird was hanging out with Western Grebes and diving frequently. It also preened occasionally and flapped once while we watched it.Call: Bird was silent.Plumage: first basic
